questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A mixture of $\mathrm{I}_2$ vapours and $\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ kept in a closed container and heated to a temperature T . After certain time the content of $\mathrm{I}_2$ decreased by $\mathbf{5 0 \%}$ compared with that of the starting mixture. The resulting mixture has a composition of 30 volume $\%$ of $\mathrm{I}_2, 10$ volume $\%$ of $\mathrm{H}_2$ and rest is HI . Select correct statement(s).
options: Initial mixture had a volume composition of $60 \% \mathrm{I}_2$ and $40 \% \mathrm{H}_2$
If volume of $\mathrm{H}_2$ content decreased by $40 \%$ compared with that of initial mixture then composition of HI in the mixture at T will be 32 volume $\%$
Initial mixture had equal moles of $\mathrm{H}_2$ and $\mathrm{I}_2$
If reaction goes to completion the volume $\%$ of $\mathrm{I}_2$ in final mixture would be 20
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{I}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{HI}(\mathrm{~g})$

To get $\mathbf{6 0}$ parts $\mathrm{HI}, 30$ parts of $\mathrm{H}_2$ and 30 parts of $\mathrm{I}_2$ should have been reacted. Initial composition should be
$\mathrm{I}_2$
$\mathbf{H}_2$
$\begin{aligned}
& 30+30=60 \% \\
& 10+30=40 \%
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For the reaction $\mathrm{PCl}_5(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{PCl}_3(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Cl}_2(\mathrm{~g})$; the forward reaction at constant temperature is favoured by
options: Adding inert gas at constant volume
Adding inert gas at constant pressure
Adding $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ at constant volume
Increasing volume of the container
questions.solution:
1. Addition of inert gas at constant pressure shifts the equilibrium in that direction which has more number of gaseous moles.
2. Increasing the volume of the container shifts the equilibrium in that direction which has more number of gaseous moles.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the reaction
$2 \mathrm{SO}_{2(\mathrm{~g})}+\mathrm{O}_{2(\mathrm{~g})} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_{3(\mathrm{~g})}+$ Heat

What will happen if 0.5 mole of Helium gas is introduced into the vessel so that the temperature and pressure remain constant?
options: the equilibrium concentration of $\mathrm{SO}_2$ increases
the equilibrium will shift to left
the equilibrium concentration of $\mathrm{O}_2$ increases
equilibrium constant will increase
questions.solution: At constant pressure, the addition of inert gas shifts the equilibrium towards larger no. of moles.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The equilibrium of which of the following reactions will not be disturbed by the addition of inert gas at constant volume.
options: $\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{I}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{HI}(\mathrm{g})$
$\mathrm{PCl}_5(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{PCl}_3(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Cl}_2(\mathrm{~g})$
$\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$
$\mathrm{N}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}(\mathrm{g})$
questions.solution: Equilibrium is not disturbed by the addition of inert gas at constant volume.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Which of the following factors will favour the backward reaction:
$\mathrm{Cl}_2(\mathrm{~g})+3 \mathrm{~F}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{ClF}_3(\mathrm{~g}) \quad \Delta \mathrm{H}=-\mathrm{ve}$
options: Addition of inert gas at constant pressure
Addition of $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ gas
Increase in temperature
Increasing the volume of the container.
questions.solution: Addition of $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ favours forward reaction, remaining backward reaction.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The thermal dissociation equilibrium of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{HS}(\mathrm{s})$ is studied under different conditions.
$\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{HS}(\mathrm{~s}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{NH}_3(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{~g})$

For this equilibrium, the correct statement(s) is (are) :
options: K depends on temperature
K is independent of the initial amount of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{HS}(s)$
K is dependent on the pressure of $\mathrm{NH}_3$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ at a given T
If pressure of $\mathrm{NH}_3(\mathrm{g})$ is increased at equilibrium than pressure of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}(\mathrm{g})$ decreases at given temperature.
questions.solution:
(A) Equilibrium constant depends on temperature.
(B) Equilibrium constant is a constant.
(C) $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{NH}_3}$ and $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}}$ depend on K .
(D) $\mathrm{K}=\mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{NH}_3} \times \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}}$
so if at equilibrium $\mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{NH}_3}$ is increased, then automatically $\mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}}$ will decrease.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: $\mathrm{A}+\mathrm{B} \rightleftarrows \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{D}$.If $\Delta G$ of the reaction isnegative and large in magnitude, then the correct statement regarding the above reaction is
options: the reaction is highly favoured towards C and D (products side)
the reaction will not favour to give products
it is highly reversible reaction
$\Delta \mathrm{G}$ large in magnitude indicates faster formation of products, C and D
questions.solution:
more negative is the dilto $a$ value, greater will be the spontaneity of the reaction.
$$
\Delta u=-R T \ln k
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A certain mass of gas $A$ is kept in a closed container where it undergoes dimerisation, according to the reaction :
$2 \mathrm{~A}(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{A}_2(\mathrm{~g})$

Assuming temperature to remain constant, it was found that the partial pressure of $\mathrm{A}_2$ gas after time t was one-fifth of the initial pressure in container. Then, select the correct statement(s) :
options: The ratio of initial total pressure to the total pressure at time tis $3: 2$.
The mole fraction of $\mathrm{A}_2$ in the vessel after time $t$ is 0.2 .
The percentage dimerisation of A upto time t is $40 \%$.
If each molecule of gas A weighs $10^{-22} \mathrm{~g}$, then the average molecular mass of the mixture in the container after time t is about 75 amu .
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \quad \begin{array}{l}
&2 A ~\longrightarrow &A_2 \\
t=0 &n &0\\
t=t &n-x &x/2\\
\end{array} \\
& \therefore \mathrm{P} \propto \text { moles } \therefore \frac{P_i}{n}=\frac{P_f}{n-\frac{x}{2}} ~...(1)
\end{aligned}$
Also, from Dalton's law, $\mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{A}_2}=\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{A}_2} \times \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{f}}$
$\therefore \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{A}_2}=\left(\frac{\frac{x}{2}}{n-\frac{x}{2}}\right) \times P_f ~...(2)$
And, $\mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{A}_2}=\frac{1}{5} \times \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}} ~...(3)$
From equations (1), (2) and (3),
$\begin{aligned}
& x=\frac{2 n}{5} \therefore \frac{P_i}{P_f}=\frac{n}{n-\frac{x}{2}}=\frac{n}{n-\frac{n}{5}}=\frac{5}{4} \\
& X_{A_2}=\left(\frac{\frac{x}{2}}{n-\frac{x}{2}}\right)=\frac{n / 5}{4 n / 5}=0.25
\end{aligned}$
$\%$ dimerisation $=\frac{x}{n} \times 100=\frac{2 n / 5}{n} \times 100=40 \%$
Mol. mass of $A=10^{-22} \times 6 \times 10^{23}=60 \mathrm{amu}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore M_{\text {avg. }}=\frac{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{A}} \times \mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{A}}+\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{A}_2} \times \mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{A}_2}}{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{A}}+\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{A}_2}} \\
& \quad=\frac{\frac{3 \mathrm{n}}{5} \times 60+\frac{\mathrm{n}}{5} \times 120}{\frac{3 \mathrm{n}}{5}+\frac{\mathrm{n}}{5}}=75 \mathrm{amu}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
